content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Carl A. Gosline 
27280 Bear Terrace 
Willits, 
CA 
95490 
Dear Mr. Gosline: 
December 18, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance, Our File No. 
1-87-306 
You have requested general information regarding three 
topics: 
(1) organization of political campaigns, (2) regulation 
of the political activities of local agency public employees, and 
(3) freedom of information provisions in California laws. 
The Commission's authority is limited to interpretation of the 
Political Reform Act. 
(Government Code sections 81000-91015.) 
The last two topics are not covered by the Political Reform Act. 
Therefore, the Commission does not have jurisdiction to interpret 
these laws. 
A city attorney, county counselor district attorney 
would be the most appropriate person to contact for interpretation 
of laws and regulations governing local public employees and 
freedom of information requirements. 
The organization of political campaigns is regulated by the 
Political Reform Act only to the extent that campaign disclosure 
statements may be required for persons who spend money for 
political purposes. 
Enclosed is a manual on campaign disclosure 
requirements. 
It should answer your questions regarding the 
specific requirements. 
If you have further questions, you may write to me or call 
(916) 322-5662.

Supplemental Complaint, a brief outline of the facts surrounding 
the dispute, and a 
copy of the unpublished Appellate decision 
regarding Ms. Jones' Petition for Writ of Mandate. 
By letter dated October 14, 1987, you responded to Mr. 
McNiece indicating that you were unable to grant his request 
contained in the above referenced letter since he did not appear 
to be authorized by the officials whose duties under the Act are 
in question.

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Carl A. Gosline 
27280 Bear Terrace 
Willits, Ca 
95490 
Dear Mr. Gosline: 
December 8, 1987 
Re: 
87-306 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on December 4, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance 
Division 
and Analysis

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for general information on three topics: (1) organization of political campaigns, (2) regulation of political activities of local agency public employees, and (3) freedom of information provisions in California laws.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has limited jurisdiction to the Political Reform Act and cannot interpret laws regarding local public employee regulations or freedom of information. The Commission can address campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act and enclosed a manual on this topic.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be a collection of multiple letters (main response dated December 18, 1987, preliminary acknowledgment dated December 8, 1987, and unrelated correspondence). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in traditional format. Extracted information from the primary FPPC response letter (Diane M. Griffiths/Bruce W. Robeck).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked for general information on three topics: (1) organization of political campaigns, (2) regulation of political activities of local agency public employees, and (3) freedom of information provisions in California laws.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has limited jurisdiction to the Political Reform Act and cannot interpret laws regarding local public employee regulations or freedom of information. The Commission can address campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act and enclosed a manual on this topic.

embedding.summary: This collection includes an FPPC response to Carl Gosline's informal advice request regarding political campaigns, employee regulations, and freedom of information. The FPPC clarifies its jurisdictional limitations and directs the requestor to appropriate local authorities for non-Political Reform Act matters.
